lines: You're angry. [short pause] I'm not going to tell you not to be. [short pause] Anger is information. [short pause] It's telling you something matters. [short pause] But right now, anger is flooding your brain with cortisol, and that's shutting down the part of you that makes good decisions.

So we're not going to act. [short pause] Not yet. [short pause] We're going to buy time. [short pause] Twenty minutes — that's how long it takes for cortisol to clear. [short pause] This exercise gets you through the worst of it.

Your breath first. [short pause] We're going to breathe in for four counts and out for eight. [short pause] The long exhale activates your vagus nerve — it's the brake pedal for your nervous system. [short pause] It physically pulls you out of fight mode.
Breathe in... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Now out, slowly... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.
In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.
In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.
In... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four.

Out... [short pause] [short pause] two... [short pause] [short pause] three... [short pause] [short pause] four... [short pause] [short pause] five... [short pause] [short pause] six... [short pause] [short pause] seven... [short pause] [short pause] eight.
Good. [short pause] Now let's find where the anger lives in your body. [short pause] Don't analyze it. [short pause] Just locate it.
Check your jaw. [short pause] Is it clenched? [short pause] Notice it. [short pause] Don't force it open. [short pause] Just notice.
Your fists. [short pause] Are they tight? [short pause] Just observe.
Your chest. [short pause] Is there pressure? [short pause] Heat?
Your stomach. [short pause] Tight? [short pause] Knotted?

Wherever you found it — direct your next exhale there. [short pause] Breathe in... [short pause] [short pause] and breathe out toward that spot. [short pause] Not to make it disappear. [short pause] Just to give it space.
One more time. [short pause] In... [short pause] [short pause] and out, toward the tension.
Be quick to listen. [short pause] Slow to speak. [short pause] Slow to anger.
Your anger is real. [short pause] What someone did — or what happened — that's real. [short pause] But you get to choose what comes next. [short pause] Not the anger. [short pause] You.
There's a difference between feeling it and following it. [short pause] Right now, you've created that space. [short pause] Stay in it.
One more breath. [short pause] In through the nose... [short pause] [short pause] long exhale out.
You're in control. [short pause] Not because the anger is gone. [short pause] Because you chose not to let it drive.
